intro:

However, I can provide information about a similar plant, Microrhynchus sarmentosus is not found and I can provide information about a similar plant  called  "Indian Tinospora" or Tinospora cordifolia. Tinospora cordifolia is a plant known for its medicinal properties, and is sometimes referred to as the "Guduchi" plant. It is a climbing shrub that is native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia.

Tinospora cordifolia is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to boost the immune system and alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.

The bioactive constituents of Tinospora cordifolia include alkaloids, glycosides, terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit a range of biological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer effects.

Tinospora cordifolia is commonly used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine and is available in various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powders. It is often used to make a traditional Ayurvedic formulation called "Guduchi Ghan Vati" or "Guduchi Rasayan", which is a concentrated extract of the plant that is used to treat a range of health conditions.
